name: MATLAB Batch On Slurm
summary: Run non-interactive MATLAB workloads on Slurm with explicit logs and license notes.
description: This skill helps users run MATLAB scripts or functions through Slurm using batch startup options while keeping license requirements, working directories, logs, and outputs explicit on shared HPC systems.

inputs.name: matlab_entrypoint
inputs.description: MATLAB script or function name to run with matlab -batch.

inputs.name: matlab_workdir
inputs.description: Directory added to the MATLAB path before running the entrypoint.

inputs.name: license_feature
inputs.description: Optional site-specific Slurm license resource or MATLAB license feature name.

inputs.name: output_dir
inputs.description: User-owned output directory for generated files.

outputs.name: slurm_log
outputs.description: Slurm stdout/stderr log for the MATLAB job.

outputs.name: matlab_log
outputs.description: Optional MATLAB logfile captured with the -logfile startup option.

outputs.name: matlab_environment_record
outputs.description: MATLAB version, license evidence, thread settings, and selected environment variables captured in logs.

outputs.name: analysis_output
outputs.description: User-defined MATLAB outputs written under the configured output directory.

examples.title: Submit a small MATLAB batch job

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced examples.
Check the Slurm wrapper shell syntax.

Replace account, partition, module, and optional license placeholders, then submit to a short test partition and confirm logs record MATLAB version, entrypoint, output directory, and license evidence.
